mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank IC above 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during post-crisis recovery periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative or flat 20-day SPX returns during the 2009-2011 recovery window with a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 195.0, accelerating inflation forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 195.0 are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in over 60% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly nonfarm payrolls confirms synchronized economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Synchronized GDPC1 and PAYEMS growth fails to generate positive 60-day SPX returns with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly nonfarm payroll growth reflects strengthening labor demand and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: PAYEMS acceleration yields negative 20-day SPX returns in late-cycle regimes with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum and factory activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year rolling window.
